data.title: Epiroc shares hit bottom of STOXX as Q2 disappoints
data.description:
** Shares in Epiroc fall 5.5% as the Swedish mining equipment maker reported quarterly results below estimates

** Its Q2 revenue reached SEK 15.28 billion ($1.58 billion), operating profit came in at SEK 2.83 billion, both missing estimates seen in LSEG poll of analysts

** Orders received decreased 7% to SEK 15.28 billion, with currency impacting negatively with 9%, Epiroc said

** The group expects demand from construction customers to remain weak short-term

** Near-term demand for mining is to remain at a high level, it said

** The stock, on track for worst day in 12 months, is the weakest performer on pan-European STOXX 600 index

($1 = 9.6653 Swedish crowns)

data.title: WhatsApp should prepare to leave the Russian market, influential lawmaker says
data.description:
WhatsApp should prepare to leave the Russian market, a lawmaker who regulates the IT sector said on Friday, warning that the messaging app owned by Meta Platforms is very likely to be put on a list of restricted software.

Russian President Vladimir Putin last month signed a law authorising the development of a state-backed messaging app integrated with government services, as Moscow strives to reduce its dependence on platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram.

Anton Gorelkin, deputy head of the lower house of parliament's information technology committee, said in a statement on Telegram that the state-backed app could gain more market share if WhatsApp leaves.

"It's time for WhatsApp to prepare to leave the Russian market," Gorelkin said, noting that Meta is designated as an extremist organisation in Russia.

The company's Facebook and Instagram social media platforms have been banned in Russia since 2022 when Moscow sent tens of thousands of troops into Ukraine.

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Russia has long sought to establish what it calls digital sovereignty by promoting home-grown services. Its push to replace foreign tech platforms became more urgent as some Western companies pulled out of the Russian market after 2022.

The Kremlin this week published a list of instructions from Putin, including an order to introduce additional restrictions on the use in Russia of software, including communication services, produced in so-called "unfriendly countries" that have imposed sanctions against Russia.

Putin gave a deadline of September 1. Gorelkin, referring to Putin's order, said WhatsApp was likely to be among those communication services restricted.

data.title: Great Wall Motors' H1 Profit Slides 10%
data.description:
Great Wall Motors saw a 10% decline in attributable profit for the six months ended June 30 to 6.34 billion yuan from an after-adjustment profit of 7.06 billion yuan in the year-ago period, a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Basic earnings per share stood at 0.74 yuan in the interim period, down from 0.83 yuan in the corresponding period of the last year.

Operating revenue for the Chinese carmaker rose by 1% to 92.4 billion yuan in the half year from 91.4 billion yuan a year prior.

data.title: Zinc Hits 15-week High
data.description:
Zinc increased to 2790.00 USD/T, the highest since April 2025.

Over the past 4 weeks, Zinc gained 5.36%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 0.37%.

data.title: GSK Shares Drop After FDA Committee Vote Leaves Cancer-Drug Comeback in Doubt
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

GSK shares fell after a Food and Drug Administration advisory committee voted against approval of the company's Blenrep drug, a setback for its bid to reintroduce the blood-cancer treatment in the U.S.

London-listed shares in GSK fell as much as 6.9% in European morning trade Friday, taking the stock down for the year to date.

The U.K. pharmaceutical company said late Thursday that the FDA oncologic drugs advisory committee voted against the overall risk and benefit profile of Blenrep combinations at the proposed dosages.

The company's submission was based on two late-stage clinical studies that evaluated the drug for patients with multiple myeloma who have received at least one prior therapy and that either don't respond to treatment or saw the disease return after being in remission. GSK had previously said the trials met their main goals.

The committee said the key safety issue with Blenrep were ocular adverse effects. It also cited uncertainty regarding the appropriateness of the proposed dosages in a briefing document on the drug.

The FDA, which generally follows the recommendations of advisory committees but isn't bound to do so, is due to make a decision by Wednesday.

GSK said it remains confident in the benefit and risk profile of Blenrep and will continue to work with the FDA. The company recently received approvals for the drug in countries including Japan, the U.K. and Switzerland.

U.S. drug regulators granted accelerated approval to Blenrep in 2020, but later revoked it after a clinical trial showed the drug--then used alone--didn't outperform an existing treatment.

The company continued to evaluate Blenrep in other settings and has been pursuing a comeback as part of its efforts to rebuild an oncology portfolio. GSK estimates Blenrep's annual peak sales could reach 3 billion pounds ($4.03 billion).

The FDA committee's vote means the FDA is unlikely to authorize Blenrep, a disappointment for investors given that this was one of the few drugs in GSK's pipeline at a late stage of development, analysts at Berenberg wrote in a note to clients.

Friday's stock losses wiped out GSK's gains earlier in the year and left its share performance in 2025 to date broadly in line with that of U.K. rival AstraZeneca.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Eni Carbon Unit Valued at Nearly $1.2 Billion in Deal With Blackrock's GIP
data.description:
(Updates with Eni's response in the fifth paragraph.)

Eni's carbon capture and storage unit is likely to be valued at about 1 billion euros ($1.16 billion) as the Italian energy major continues to negotiate a deal with BlackRock to sell nearly half of the unit, Bloomberg News said Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Eni has been in exclusive talks with BlackRock to sell a 49.99% stake in CCUS Holding since May, the people said, adding that the discussions are ongoing and valuations can still change, according to the report.

If a deal is reached between the two companies, which the people said may happen by the end of summer, it would represent another step in Chief Executive Officer Claudio Descalzi's plan to reduce risk by spinning off units while maintaining a stake, the report said.

In June, Eni sold a 20% stake in its renewable arm Plenitude to Ares Alternative Credit Management for about 2 billion euros, Bloomberg reported.

An Eni spokesperson declined to comment, while BlackRock did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Bitcoin Price, XRP Gain as Genius Act Passes Congress. Trump Has a Bigger Plan for Cryptos. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Bitcoin wavered while altcoins jumped Friday after the crypto industry notched a major regulatory win in the U.S., and a report that 401(k) plans could soon include crypto investments as well.

The price of the world's largest crypto Bitcoin was wavering early Friday but was on a downward trend over the last few hours. It traded up 0.2% over the past 24 hours to $118,600, according to CoinDesk data but is down from a 24-hour high of nearly $121,000.

Bitcoin hit a record high of $122,658 earlier this week amid optimism around Congress considering several crypto bills. Ultimately, analysts say that the proposed laws could transform the crypto sector and make it more investible, especially for institutions.

Meanwhile, altcoins were jumping. XRP gained around 5% to $3.40, briefly surging to a new all-time high of $3.64. Ether rose 4% and Solana climbed 1.3%.

The House of Representatives late Thursday passed the GENIUS Act, a bill that would regulate stablecoins, meaning it could soon be signed into law by President Donald Trump. The House also voted in favor of the Digital Markets Clarity Act.

The Clarity Act is in many ways the more important bill to the larger crypto industry as it would address the confusion over whether cryptos are commodities or securities, and what regulator would oversee them. It's also a more complex effort and still needs to pass the Senate.

Separately, Trump is preparing to sign an executive order as soon as this week to open up 401(k) plans to alternative investments, including cryptos, the Financial Times reported late Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

6.             See Rule 2.5(d) of Part A of the Rules.

7.             If details included in a disclosure under Rule 8 are incorrect, they should be corrected as soon as practicable in a subsequent disclosure. Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rule 8 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to "the Rules" are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.

data.title: Northern Dynasty Minerals Seeks Summary Judgment on Vetoed Copper Mining Project After Failed EPA Talks
data.description:
Northern Dynasty Minerals said Thursday that the company and its US-based subsidiary Pebble have filed a motion in Federal District Court in Alaska.

The motion is seeking a summary judgment briefing schedule in the company's pending litigation regarding the lawfulness of a veto of a copper mining project by the administration of former US President Joe Biden.

Chief Executive Ron Thiessen said the company has not been able to reach a settlement with the US Environmental Protection Agency regarding the project.

"We asked the court to set a briefing schedule for summary judgment motions, as we now believe that will be the quickest, most direct avenue to get the veto removed," he said.

data.title: Vault Ventures Purchases Additional Digital Assets
data.description:
Vault Ventures (VULT.AQ) said Friday that it purchased additional Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana.

The UK-based early-stage technology businesses developer purchased 0.21 Bitcoin, 58.4513 Ethereum, and 59.00 Solana at an average per asset purchase price of 88,052.48 pounds sterling, 2,553.66 pounds, and 134.02 pounds, respectively.

The new purchases bring the total number of digital assets owned by Vault Ventures to 4.00 Bitcoin, 711.93 Ethereum, and 2,200.32 Solana.

data.title: Crystal Amber Fund Reviews Future Strategy, Return Capital to Shareholders
data.description:
Crystal Amber Fund said Friday it is consulting with shareholders and advisers on its strategy for its future structure and plans to return capital to shareholders as it received cash proceeds from the takeover of De La Rue.

The activist fund received a final payment of 18 million pounds sterling as Atlas Holdings completed its acquisition of the banknote printing group De La Rue, in which Crystal Amber Fund previously held a stake.

The London-listed company said its planned return of capital will include a share buyback program and potential distributions. It added that it remains focused on maximizing value from its other investments.

data.title: Nvidia Insider Sold Shares Worth $8,142,671, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Kress Colette, Executive Vice President & Chief Financial Officer, on July 15, 2025, sold 47,650 shares in Nvidia for $8,142,671. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Kress Colette has control over a total of 5,415,862 common shares of the company, with 2,984,516 shares held directly and 2,431,346 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000158867025000003/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1752799565.xml
